title: Telangana launches Indiramma Housing scheme in Hyderabad; releases eligibility, application procedure, beneficiary contribution details
content: The flats with built up area of 528 sft will be built in multi-storey towers. While Telangana government would provide subsidy of ₹5 lakh, tentative contribution of beneficiary is ₹6 lakh. A flat will be spread over 400 sft.

title: March to Parliament: Protesters’ anger extends beyond paper leaks
content: One of the many common threads connecting those who faced police lathis was that they had voted for the BJP in the past or came from families that had traditionally supported the BJP, making their disappointment more acute

title: Calcutta High Court refuses Trinamools Congress’ plea to operate bank accounts frozen by ED
content: Bidhannagar Police had earlier frozen the same three accounts, but a different bench of the HC allowed Mamata Banerjee-led faction to use money, about ₹440 crore, under judicial oversight.

title: Trinamool factions, Congress plan show of strength in Kolkata on Martyrs’ Day
content: Each group claims to be the ‘original’ flagbearer of Martyrs’ Day; all three meetings have been arranged within a 3 km radius; senior leaders have been trying to pull in crowds

title: Cauvery Water Management Authority is meeting on Wednesday amid a growing deficit in water realisation by Tamil Nadu
content: Since June 1, the start of the current water year, four reservoirs of the Cauvery basin have received around 30 tmc ft, whereas Tamil Nadu has not even received four tmc ft, says an expert in Chennai

title: Modi is India’s most ‘anti-youth PM’, says Rahul Gandhi
content: ‘Tear-gassing students, lathi-charging students is not democratic...They have legitimate demands. The Prime Minister should accept these demands and do something about it,’ the Congress leader says

title: Two long-awaited Hyderabad projects near finish line; launch expected next month
content: GHMC is preparing to partially open the new high-level bridge across the Musi river at Moosarambagh, restoring a key link that has remained shut since the old causeway was demolished last year; Nalgonda Crossroads-Owaisi Hospital elevated corridor is also nearing completion

title: Tamil Nadu scales up ABHA creation, 2.54 crore people covered so far
content: ABHA, a 14-digit unique health identifier created using an Aadhaar number, is a key feature of the Union government’s Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), launched in 2021, enabling a digital health records ecosystem

title: AISA activists end 23-day hunger strike, to continue ‘other forms of struggle’
content: One activist criticises government decision to lathi-charge protesters on Monday, saying that the government and police are not “able to understand that this fight is for their children”

title: Four Indians killed, one injured in attack on ship leaving Ukraine’s Odesa, MEA says
content: The MEA extended condolences to the families of the deceased and wished the injured Indian national a speedy recovery

title: Govt. to permit Kaleshwaram barrages’ operation only after experts’ nod: Uttam
content: Minister for Irrigation says all tests on barrages nearing completion; focus is on designs of rehabilitation

title: Govt. will hold talks with tribal organisations on key issues soon, says Naidu
content: Coalition government is exploring all legally viable alternatives to ensure that tribal communities continue to receive the intended benefits under G.O. 3 struck down by SC, asserts Chandrababu Naidu

title: Former CorroHealth employees say compensation promise a huge relief
content: The company has agreed to pay five months’ gross salary, in addition to the full and final settlement amount paid to the employees earlier

title: ₹207 crore boost for tourism projects in A.P. : Union Minister
content: Projects worth ₹127.40 crore and ₹79.37 crore were sanctioned under Swadesh Darshan 2.0 and PRASHAD schemes respectively for the development of the tourism sector in Andhra Pradesh

title: Current politics will not allow a Kapu leader to become CM of A.P., says MLC Somu Veerraju
content: It's time to introspect for Kapu leaders, says the former BJP State unit president; community leaders give a call for an action plan to revive the Kapu movement to achieve reservation in the State, saying it will be a real tribute to Mudragada Padmanabham

title: NEET-UG: Submitting fake OMR sheets for scrutiny will invite legal action, says NTA
content: The agency says it had got notice that images of OMR answer sheets said to be the “correct” record of certain candidates who appeared in NEET(UG) 2026 were being circulated on social media

title: Don’t surrender Thekkinkadu Maidan to communal forces, says CPI
content: Seeks all-party meeting to preserve Maidan as a public space; poet K.G. Sankara Pillai warns against shrinking democratic spaces

title: In literature, there are no Left or Right camps, only the side of truth: Speaker
content: Thiruvanchiyoor Radhakrishnan presents Anganam Shamsuddeen Memorial Literary Awards; writer Vaishakhan receives Distinguished Literary Service honour

title: Trainee IPS officer, booked for sexual assault, collapses after alcohol consumption
content: Meanwhile, Hyderabad police said they had no information about the hospital incident and that no separate case had been registered in connection with it.

title: Democracy being trampled in the streets of Delhi: Leader of the Opposition Pinarayi Vijayan
content: He calls for a strong, nation-wide protests against the government’s failure to address serious irregularities in national-level entrance examinations.

title: State clears ₹522 crore power works for 2027 Godavari Pushkaralu
content: RDSS contracts extended till March 2027; ₹177 crore SCADA upgrade also cleared to strengthen power distribution network ahead of the mega religious event.

title: Church does not receive any foreign funds in personal accounts, KCBC
content: Catholic Church leadership in Kerala reiterates concerns over Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026

title: Special court in Mumbai sentences 44 Somalian pirates to life imprisonment; praises Navy for prompt action
content: In March 2024, a hijacking alert was received from Iranian flagged FV Al Kambar, leading to the arrest of nine Somalian nationals after INS Trushul and Sumedha responded

title: Fishers allege Mamallan reservoir plan ignores tidal inundation risks
content: They argued that the WRD’s proposal and the technical assessment by the IIT-M, had not adequately assessed this risk; they further said that converting the marsh into a reservoir would eliminate about 4,375 acres of natural flood storage, increasing flood risk in nearby areas

title: Nellore children’s park to feature zip line, cycling track soon, says Minister
content: Addressing a review meeting, Ponguru Narayana says all parks in the city will be modernised in a phased manner to meet the needs of the public

title: Rajya Sabha adjourned six times over paper leak
content: Opposition shouts slogans when three members who switched from the Trinamool to the BJP took oath; Congress members heckle BJP leader Mahesh Kewat, the member from Madhya Pradesh, and shout “seat chori”

title: Centre introduces Bill to raise strength of judges in Supreme Court to 38
content: The Bill seeks to replace an ordinance promulgated by the President in May to increase the strength of judges in the top court from 34 to 38, including the Chief Justice of India

title: Stake sale at Vizhinjam: empowered panel may take 3-4 months to submit its report
content: The committee, headed by the Chief Secretary, has yet to examine the nuances of the stake sale or complete the exercise

title: Pothundy double murder case: a death penalty verdict with rare focus on rehabilitating victims’ family
content: Court directs District Legal Services Authority, Palakkad, to initiate rehabilitation measures for orphaned children of the victims; legal experts say such a specific direction was rare in criminal judgments

title: CJP Leaders Meet Union Minister JP Nadda, Submit Letter Of Demands
content: CJP spokesperson Saurav Das in a post on X said that he and another spokesperson, Ashutosh Ranka, met Union minister JP Nadda at his house for 10 minutes

title: 4 Of Family Swept Away In Assam Flash Floods, Villagers Allege Delay
content: According to local residents, heavy rainfall triggered a sudden rise in the water level, inundating several parts of the village. As floodwaters entered the house of Rohini Dubara, the family attempted to move to a safer location.

title: Security Measures To Talks: How Centre Handled Escalation Of CJP Protest
content: Sources said Home Minister Amit Shah took the lead in reviewing the situation and discussing the way forward.

title: 4 Indians Killed, 1 Critical After Attack On Ship At Odesa Port In Ukraine
content: The ship, MV Golden Leo, had 17 crew members on board at the time of the attack, including five Indian nationals.

title: Pak's "Victim Card" On Indus Waters Can't Change India's Decision: Sources
content: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) will never function for India in its present form and India would consider a renegotiation only if Pakistan takes credible steps to abjure terrorism

title: Explained: Why Punjab's MGNREGA Protest Is A Political Test For AAP
content: Thousands of MGNREGA employees and workers from all 23 Punjab districts and nearly 250 blocks gathered in Khanna on Monday to press for their long-pending demands.

title: Over 1.7 Lakh People Affected In Assam Floods, Army Launches 'Operation Jal Rahat'
content: Floods in Assam have affected over 1.7 lakh people across 12 districts, 30 revenue circles and 580 villages.

title: 60,000 People Duped Of Rs 100 Crore In Job Scam, 6 Arrested In UP
content: A joint team of the Cyber Cell, Crime Branch and Hanumant Vihar police arrested six alleged members of the syndicate during a raid at Mangalam garden on Arra Road in Barra, police said.

title: Get 10-Year Tax Waiver In Delhi By Scrapping Old Commercial Vehicle
content: The Delhi government says around 2.07 lakh truck and bus owners across the National Capital Region could benefit. The package includes tax exemptions, registration-fee waivers, interest support and manufacturer discounts.

title: Class 11 Student Stabbed To Death After Quarrel In Delhi's Kalkaji: Police
content: According to police, a PCR call regarding a quarrel among school students was received at 2.23 pm. A police team rushed to the spot and found the victim lying with stab injuries.

title: Khalil Al-Hayya: Man Who Led Gaza Ceasefire Talks Will Now Lead Hamas
content: Al-Hayya rose steadily in prominence as senior Hamas figures were killed one after another, including Ismail Haniyeh, assassinated in Tehran in July 2024.

title: On Camera, 3 Men Loot Jewellery Shop In Maharashtra
content: To discourage witnesses from intervening, they fired shots in the air before speeding away on their motorcycle.

title: Nepal Man Beats Father To Death With Iron Rod In Bengaluru Apartment Complex
content: The father had joined as a security guard at an apartment complex on Friday and the son went there in search of a job two days later.

title: "Tear-Gassing, Lathicharging Students Not India's Way": Rahul Gandhi On CJP March
content: The Delhi Police fired tear gas and baton-charged protesters on Monday as they tried to march on parliament to demand the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over irregularities in major examinations, including question paper leaks and technical glitches.

title: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: "Protests To Continue," Says CJP After Meeting With JP Nadda
content: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: Union minister JP Nadda and CJP members Saurav Das and Ashutosh Ranka met in Delhi.

title: 4 Die After Entering Abandoned Well To Rescue Calf In Haryana Village
content: Jhajjar's Police Commissioner Rajshree Singh told reporters that rescue efforts were launched and the men were brought out and taken to hospital, but they were declared dead.

title: "I Will Continue My Fast": Sonam Wangchuk After Government Meets Protesters
content: Wangchuk's wife, Gitanjali Angmo, shared on social media a handwritten message from her husband that offered solidarity to the protesters under the banner of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)

title: RBI Flags Conflict, Monsoon Risks. These May Already Be Pushing Inflation
content: The RBI has already revised its inflation outlook upward. In its June monetary policy, the central bank raised its FY27 CPI inflation forecast to 5.1 per cent from 4.6 per cent projected earlier.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Not Just Jets, China Is Developing A '5-Piece' Carrier Set, And India Should Worry
content: China is now the only state serially producing two distinct stealth fighter families simultaneously, with some estimates projecting that advanced-fighter output across all jet programmes could reach 300-400 aircraft annually by 2027.

title: US Says Cuba Infiltrated "Highest Reaches" Of American Government
content: A US State Department report alleged that Cuba's decades-long espionage campaign was "one of the most durable and damaging foreign intelligence penetrations in American history."

title: Bhatti Vikramarka inaugurates Integrated Oil Palm Complex, calls oil palm an assured source of income even during drought

title: 'Thank you, India!': Argentina envoy thanks Indian fans after World Cup heartbreak
content: Argentina's Ambassador expressed gratitude to Indian football fans for their support.  The Albiceleste reached the FIFA World Cup final but lost to Spain.  Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in extra time for Spain.  Emiliano Martinez made several crucial saves for the Argentine team.  This gesture highlights the strong connection between India and Argentine football.

title: Sergey Brin's invention saved my vision, analyst says as Sanders targets $82M tax push
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is spending $82 million to defeat California's Proposition 40, a one-time 5% wealth tax on billionaires. Senator Bernie Sanders slammed the move as a "moral obscenity," saying Brin would rather see low-income Americans suffer. But policy analyst Patrick Hedger defended him, crediting Brin's search engine with helping find a doctor who saved his vision after four others failed.

title: Former England captain and two-time Ballon d'Or winner Kevin Keegan dies at 75
content: English football icon Kevin Keegan has passed away at seventy-five years old. He was a two-time Ballon d'Or winner and a Liverpool European Cup champion. Keegan also enjoyed a successful managerial career with Newcastle United and England. His influence extended beyond football into popular culture during the 1970s. He leaves behind a significant legacy as a player and manager.

title: E20 petrol debate: Govt says 'no verified evidence' of widespread engine failure
content: Minister of State for Petroleum and Natural Gas Suresh Gopi said the shift to E20 fuel has been "phased, calibrated and evidence-based," noting that the ethanol blending rate was gradually raised from 1.53 per cent in 2013-14 to 20 per cent in 2025-26 following "scientific evaluation and stakeholder consultations at every stage."

title: 'Total disgrace': Argentina branded 'classless' as post-final conduct sparks outrage
content: Argentina faced criticism for their behavior after losing the World Cup final.  Players did not remain on the podium during Spain's trophy presentation.  This action drew condemnation from many football community members.  Earlier, a confrontation occurred between players from both teams on the field.  The team's overall tournament performance also faced scrutiny from some observers.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: How electrical hazards are killing children, workers and families
content: Recent tragedies highlight electrical safety failures in public spaces and institutions.  A teenager died at a fair while a child perished in a Delhi park.  An engineer's death in Noida prompts criminal proceedings against the electricity department.  Ghaziabad schools face high-tension threats, and a Chennai man died saving family.  Four family members died in Maharashtra village, prompting safety audit demands.

title: Tiny colour changes in wild snapdragons may decide which genes survive
content: Wild snapdragons showcase an impressive ability to adjust their petal colors with the help of seven genes, aimed at attracting bees. These vibrant flowers utilize this precise color calibration as visual cues for their pollinators. Researchers discovered that these genetic variations create subtle yet significant differences, which are critical for survival in narrow hybrid zones where less effective colors are less likely to thrive.

title: Underwater drones find ‘dead’ West African coral reef alive after decades
content: After being presumed extinct for sixty years, a remarkable coral reef off West Africa has been rediscovered, thriving with an array of fish and coral species. This vibrant mesophotic coral ecosystem thrives in low-light environments beneath the ocean's surface. Researchers speculate that similar hidden reefs could exist along the West African coastline, providing valuable insights into the evolutionary history of the region's marine life and past environmental changes.

title: US judge to Meta's laid off employees: Cannot stop the company from job cuts
content: A US judge has allowed Meta Platforms to proceed with planned layoffs of 26 employees. These employees had sued the company alleging AI-driven discrimination in job cuts. The judge declined an emergency request to halt the layoffs while claims proceed. He stated the court may reconsider if new evidence emerges about AI usage. Meta denies wrongdoing and states human decisions guided the workforce reduction.

title: Vacant for decades? Why the government can't simply take over abandoned properties
content: In India, the absence of a comprehensive law governing abandoned buildings leads to fragmented regulations across various legislative acts. Municipal authorities hold the authority to inspect and classify buildings as dangerous. Owners are notified to undertake necessary repairs or secure their properties, with demolition a potential option in cases of imminent risk. Both Maharashtra and Delhi enforce mandatory structural evaluations for older buildings.

title: Maple Leafs could trade $60M Defenseman Morgan Rielly before 2026-27 season
content: Morgan Rielly trade speculation continues to swirl around the Toronto Maple Leafs, and several NHL insiders believe the veteran defenseman could be moved before the start of the 2026-27 season. Rielly has yet to waive his no-movement clause, but interest from several teams and Toronto’s reported willingness to get value in return has kept trade talk alive all offseason.

title: Raiders linked to blockbuster trade for Vikings’ $140M star WR Justin Jefferson
content: Ahead of the new season, the Minnesota Vikings' popular wide receiver Justin jefferson's name has floated among trade speculations. Amid this, the Las Vegas Raiders have emerged as a potential landing spot for him.

title: How Lamine Yamal’s romance turned Inés García into Spain’s new social media star
content: Inés García Santos is a 21-year-old Spanish fashion and beauty influencer from Seville who built a sizeable digital following through fashion, lifestyle, and beauty content before her relationship with Lamine Yamal thrust her into the global spotlight. The couple met through social media and confirmed their relationship publicly in May 2026 at Barcelona's La Liga celebration. García flew to the United States despite her known fear of flying to support Yamal throughout Spain's 2026 World Cup campaign, attending every match and documenting the journey with her growing audience of over 1.4 million Instagram followers and 1.7 million TikTok followers.

title: From Tejas rejection to 5th-gen fighter jet hopes: Can Kaveri 2.0 finally deliver?
content: India's Kaveri jet engine project shows signs of revival after years of delays. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh urged scientists to develop sixth-generation fighter engines soon. Technologies from the Kaveri program will support future combat aircraft and unmanned vehicles. India continues to rely on imported GE engines for its current fighter fleet. This renewed focus aims for self-reliance in military aviation propulsion systems.

title: India's forex boost: RBI's swap scheme raises $20.72 billion so far
content: The Reserve Bank of India's special swap facility attracted $20.72 billion by July 17.  Most of this money arrived through Foreign Currency Non-Resident deposits.  Overseas Foreign Currency Borrowings and External Commercial Borrowings also contributed to inflows.  This facility was launched to strengthen India's balance of payments and encourage capital.  Banks anticipate a significant rise in FCNR(B) deposits before the September deadline.

title: After beating Argentina, Spain return to Madrid with FIFA World Cup 2026 trophy
content: Spain's national football team returned home after winning the FIFA World Cup 2026.  The team landed in Madrid and will celebrate with supporters on July 20.  Spain secured their second World Cup title by defeating Argentina 1-0 in the final.  Ferran Torres scored the winning goal in extra time after a cross from Pedro Porro.  Goalkeeper Emiliano Martinez made a record eleven saves for Argentina during the match.

title: Shabana Azmi falls ill during protests at Jantar Mantar, safely escorted to the security cabin
content: Veteran actor Shabana Azmi reportedly fell ill during a protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar. She appeared visibly weak and was escorted to a security cabin for assistance. The actor had visited the protest site to express solidarity with the ongoing agitation. She spoke about the right to peaceful protest and non-violence. Several other celebrities have also voiced their support for the movement.

title: Coca-Cola adds fizz to India bottling unit IPO plan, appoints JPMorgan & Citi as bankers
content: Coca-Cola's Indian bottling unit IPO plans are advancing. JPMorgan and Citi have been appointed to manage the proposed 2027 listing. The company is also exploring the sale of a portion of its stake. This move aligns with Coca-Cola's global asset-light strategy. Hindustan Coca-Cola Beverages is a leading player in India's soft drinks market.

title: 'Messi, Messi!': Argentina fans honour their captain as Spain lift FIFA World Cup trophy
content: Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final after extra time. Lionel Messi's final World Cup match ended in heartbreak for the legendary player. Thousands of Argentine fans showed unwavering support for Messi despite the disappointing loss. Spain's disciplined performance denied Messi opportunities to influence the crucial match. The defeat concluded Argentina's bid to defend their World Cup title successfully.

title: Naseeruddin Shah admits neglecting first family, calls Ratna Pathak his true love
content: Naseeruddin Shah candidly reflects on his first marriage to Purveen Murad. He admits to being an immature husband and a distant father in his early twenties. Parenthood exposed cracks in their marriage, and he felt emotionally detached. His focus shifted to his acting ambitions, leading to marital breakdown. He later met and married Ratna Pathak Shah after a lengthy divorce process.

title: 'I will continue my fast': Wangchuk says peaceful protesters dealt with brutality
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike after witnessing police actions against protestors. He demands that youth leaders meet parliamentarians or he be allowed to meet them. Wangchuk appeals to the government and police to let students present their grievances. Thousands marched to Parliament demanding the Education Minister's resignation over examination irregularities. Police used tear gas and force, detaining several protestors during the march.

title: No waiver, no worries: India double downs on Russian oil in July
content: Industry experts say India's crude procurement pattern during the US-Iran conflict reflects a flexible and well-diversified sourcing strategy. India’s crude oil imports from Russia in July so far have averaged at around 2.45 million barrels per day.

title: DGCA's new eGCA platform aims to make aviation incident reporting faster
content: The DGCA is developing a digital platform for faster aviation incident reporting. This initiative aims to enhance the country's aviation safety oversight capabilities. Airfare pricing is determined by dynamic supply and demand forces. The government generally avoids regulating airfares to maintain market competitiveness. However, it intervenes in exceptional circumstances to ensure fair pricing.

title: 12 workers trapped in Sikkim tunnel after landslide blocks entrance
content: Twelve workers are trapped inside a Sikkim tunnel after a landslide blocked its entrance. A suspected gas leak inside the Samardung tunnel complicated ongoing rescue operations. Rescue teams are equipped with gas masks to reach the trapped individuals. Ambulances are on standby as efforts continue to free the workers. The tunnel is part of a highway construction project in Namchi district.

title: 'No case': HC rejects plea of Mamata faction to operate TMC accounts worth Rs 440cr
content: The Calcutta high court denied interim relief to the Trinamool Congress faction. Three party bank accounts frozen by the Enforcement Directorate will remain inaccessible. The court found no prima facie case for the petitioners' request. The Enforcement Directorate is investigating alleged money laundering and dishonest financial transactions. The matter will be heard again on August 26.

title: Spain's Rodri scripts history, joins football's most exclusive club after WC win
content: Rodri achieved a rare football honour by winning the World Cup 2026 with Spain. He joined legends Gerd Müller, Franz Beckenbauer, and Zinedine Zidane with this achievement. Rodri also secured the World Cup Golden Ball as the tournament's best player. His exceptional passing and defensive contributions were vital for Spain's victory. This win cements Rodri's status among football's all-time great midfielders.

title: ‘Avengers: Doomsday’ trailer out: RDJ’s Doctor Doom steals the spotlight
content: The highly anticipated Avengers: Doomsday trailer has been officially released by the Russo Brothers.  This new installment features the Fantastic Four and original Avengers preparing for battle.  Thor rallies the heroes, emphasizing the importance of unity against a formidable threat.  The trailer showcases intense action and emotional storytelling, quickly becoming a fan favorite.  Avengers: Doomsday is scheduled for a December 18, 2026 release date.

title: World’s top 10 costliest rental markets: Where do Mumbai and Delhi rank
content: Around the globe, rental prices fluctuate widely, showcasing vast disparities among major cities. New York and Zurich top the charts, being the priciest for rentals. In contrast, Mumbai and Bengaluru emerge as affordable options by global standards. When it comes to property purchases, Hong Kong reigns as the most expensive. However, the overall appeal and affordability of a city transcend just housing costs.

title: Ashwin points to selection bias, explains why Rohit and Virat are 'impossible to drop'
content: Ravichandran Ashwin stated dropping Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli is nearly impossible. Their extraordinary achievements and immense fan following make them undroppable players. Ashwin highlighted the disparity in how batters and bowlers are perceived in Indian cricket. Bowlers receive less public backing and face limited criticism when dropped. This contrasts sharply with the potential uproar from dropping batting icons.

title: Bengaluru to host India-U.S. Civil Space Joint Working Group meet in August

title: 'Got no commitments': CJP leaders handover 3 demands to JP Nadda; minister says end 'sit-in'
content: Cockroach Janata Party leaders met Union minister JP Nadda regarding the NEET-UG paper leak case. Protesters marched towards Parliament demanding accountability and specific government actions. The Union minister sought time to consult leadership on the party's demands. Meanwhile, Union Home Minister Amit Shah met the Education Minister at Parliament. Police used mild force and tear gas as demonstrators attempted to breach barricades.

title: WATCH: Spain players form guard of honour for Messi after World Cup win
content: Spain secured the FIFA World Cup 2026 title after a hard-fought extra-time victory over Argentina.  The Spanish team formed a guard of honour for Lionel Messi and his teammates.  Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal which handed La Roja their second World Cup.  Messi's quest for a second World Cup title ended as Argentina's reign concluded.  Spain's disciplined defence successfully contained the legendary Argentine captain throughout the match.

title: Europe's extreme heat is threatening Italy's 800-year-old Parmesan tradition
content: Europe faces increasingly severe heatwaves, impacting infrastructure and public health significantly.  Temperatures are rising nearly twice the global average, causing widespread concern.  This extreme heat directly affects milk production for Parmigiano Reggiano cheese.  Farmers struggle to maintain cow health and feed supplies due to prolonged heat.  The vital Italian cheese industry faces economic and food security challenges.
